title: Security Hardening — Multisig, Timelock, Role Separation, and Integrity Verification
summary: Address the 7 remaining security findings from the Day 1 audit: deploy a multisig wallet (SEC-01), add a timelock to UUPS upgrades (SEC-02), implement role-based access control (SEC-01), enforce evaluation-before-recording (SEC-06), add system prompt integrity verification (SEC-07), enforce divergence recording (SEC-08), add canonical JSON hashing (SEC-10), and protect corpus weights from ungovernanced modification (SEC-11).
body:
The Day 1 security audit produced 12 findings. Four were fixed immediately in code (SEC-03 prompt injection mitigation, SEC-04 path traversal validation, SEC-05 corpus integrity check, SEC-09 priority whitelist). SEC-12 (annual heartbeat) was resolved via the governance.yml cron schedule. The remaining 7 findings require smart contract changes, infrastructure deployment, or governance enforcement mechanisms that constitute governance acts under the Operating Agreement.

This proposal bundles the remaining findings into three phases: Phase 1 addresses the two CRITICAL items (single-key sovereignty and no upgrade timelock) which represent existential risk to the governance record. Phase 2 addresses the three HIGH items (evaluation enforcement, divergence enforcement, prompt integrity) which represent governance bypass risks. Phase 3 addresses the two MEDIUM items (canonical JSON hashing, corpus weight protection) which represent data integrity risks.

The phased approach ensures the highest-risk items are resolved first while allowing lower-priority items to be implemented without blocking critical infrastructure.

proposedChanges.change: Phase 1: Deploy Gnosis Safe multisig (2-of-3 initially, expandable). Deploy OpenZeppelin TimelockController with 7-day minimum delay for contract upgrades. Implement AccessControl with roles: PROPOSAL_SUBMITTER, DECISION_RECORDER, EXECUTION_ATTESTER, DIVERGENCE_RECORDER. Transfer contract ownership from EOA to multisig+timelock.

Phase 2a (SEC-06): Add on-chain linkage between evaluation output and recorded decisions. Options: require commit SHA in recordDecision(), or add verifyDecision() that checks keccak256 of raw JSON matches stored hash.

Phase 2b (SEC-08): Enforce divergence recording. Require either attestation OR divergence for each decision before a configurable timeout. Add verify.py check comparing executed state against recorded decisions.

Phase 2c (SEC-07): Add system prompt hash verification. Maintain expected prompt hashes in a config file. evaluate.py verifies prompt file hash before loading. Add CI check requiring governance decision for prompt file changes.

Phase 3a (SEC-10): Implement canonical JSON serialization for all hashing operations. Use json.dumps(data, sort_keys=True, separators=(',', ':'), ensure_ascii=True). Update evaluate.py, verify.py, and document canonical format. Must not break existing hash records.

Phase 3b (SEC-11): Protect corpus weights.json from ungovernanced modification. Add CI check: if weights.json is modified, require a corresponding governance decision reference. Record weights hash in evaluate.py output for audit trail.

milestones.description: Deploy Gnosis Safe multisig wallet (2-of-3, with HE wallet as one signer)
Deploy TimelockController with 7-day minimum delay, connected to UUPS proxy upgrade path
Implement AccessControl with PROPOSAL_SUBMITTER, DECISION_RECORDER, EXECUTION_ATTESTER, DIVERGENCE_RECORDER roles
Transfer contract ownership from EOA to multisig+timelock
Add evaluation-to-decision linkage: recordDecision requires commit SHA or on-chain verification of evaluation hash
Enforce divergence protocol: require attestation OR divergence for each decision within configurable timeout
System prompt integrity verification: hash check before evaluation, CI enforcement for prompt changes
Implement canonical JSON serialization for all hashing operations without breaking existing records
CI enforcement: weights.json changes require governance decision reference, weights hash recorded in evaluation output

1.name: Critical — Key Management & Upgrade Protection
1.description: Multisig, timelock, RBAC, ownership transfer. Addresses SEC-01 and SEC-02.

2.name: High — Governance Enforcement
2.description: Evaluation enforcement, divergence enforcement, prompt integrity. Addresses SEC-06, SEC-07, SEC-08.

3.name: Medium — Data Integrity
3.description: Canonical JSON hashing, corpus weight protection. Addresses SEC-10, SEC-11.

estimatedCost: Gas for Safe deployment + TimelockController + contract upgrade. Estimated < $5 on Base L2.
